## MINIX 3 PROCESS & PRIVILEGE TABLE STRUCTURES — COMPREHENSIVE REFERENCE

### 1. STRUCT PROC (minix/kernel/proc.h:22-137)

Complete field listing with types and purposes:

| Line | Field | Type | Purpose |
|------|-------|------|---------|
| 23 | `p_reg` | `struct stackframe_s` | Process register state (CPU registers at trap/context save) |
| 24 | `p_seg` | `struct segframe` | Segment/memory context (page table root, FPU state, trap style) |
| 25 | `p_nr` | `proc_nr_t` (int) | Process table entry number for fast access |
| 26 | `p_priv` | `struct priv *` | Pointer to privilege structure (shared by user processes, unique per system process) |
| 27 | `p_rts_flags` | `volatile u32_t` | Runtime state flags; process runnable iff == 0 |
| 28 | `p_misc_flags` | `volatile u32_t` | Miscellaneous flags (do not suspend process) |
| 30 | `p_priority` | `char` | Current scheduling priority |
| 31 | `p_cpu_time_left` | `u64_t` | Time (in cycles) remaining for this quantum |
| 32-33 | `p_quantum_size_ms` | `unsigned` | Assigned time quantum in milliseconds (marked FIXME—subject to removal) |
| 34 | `p_scheduler` | `struct proc *` | Pointer to scheduler process; NULL means kernel scheduler |
| 35 | `p_cpu` | `unsigned` | Which CPU process is running on |
| 37-44 | `p_cpu_mask[BITMAP_CHUNKS(CONFIG_MAX_CPUS)]` | `bitchunk_t[]` | SMP: CPUs allowed to run this process |
| 40-44 | `p_stale_tlb[BITMAP_CHUNKS(CONFIG_MAX_CPUS)]` | `bitchunk_t[]` | SMP: CPUs with possibly stale TLB entries from this process |
| 49 | `p_accounting.enter_queue` | `u64_t` | Cycle count when enqueued |
| 50 | `p_accounting.time_in_queue` | `u64_t` | Total time spent in queue |
| 51 | `p_accounting.dequeues` | `unsigned long` | Counter: dequeue events |
| 52 | `p_accounting.ipc_sync` | `unsigned long` | Counter: synchronous IPC calls |
| 53 | `p_accounting.ipc_async` | `unsigned long` | Counter: asynchronous IPC calls |
| 54 | `p_accounting.preempted` | `unsigned long` | Counter: preemption events |
| 57 | `p_dequeued` | `clock_t` | Uptime (ticks) when last dequeued |
| 59 | `p_user_time` | `clock_t` | User-mode time accumulated (ticks) |
| 60 | `p_sys_time` | `clock_t` | Kernel-mode time accumulated (ticks) |
| 62 | `p_virt_left` | `clock_t` | Process-virtual timer ticks remaining |
| 63 | `p_prof_left` | `clock_t` | Profile timer ticks remaining |
| 65 | `p_cycles` | `u64_t` | Total CPU cycles consumed |
| 66 | `p_kcall_cycles` | `u64_t` | Cycles consumed in kernel calls |
| 67 | `p_kipc_cycles` | `u64_t` | Cycles consumed in IPC |
| 69 | `p_tick_cycles` | `u64_t` | Cycles accumulated toward next clock tick |
| 70 | `p_cpuavg` | `struct cpuavg` | Running CPU utilization average for ps(1) |
| 72 | `p_nextready` | `struct proc *` | Link to next ready process (scheduling queue) |
| 73 | `p_caller_q` | `struct proc *` | Head of queue: processes wanting to send to this one |
| 74 | `p_q_link` | `struct proc *` | Link in sender queue (next process blocking on this receiver) |
| 75 | `p_getfrom_e` | `endpoint_t` | Receive from whom? (RECEIVING or SENDREC state) |
| 76 | `p_sendto_e` | `endpoint_t` | Send to whom? (SENDING state) |
| 78 | `p_pending` | `sigset_t` | Bitmap of pending kernel signals |
| 80 | `p_name[PROC_NAME_LEN]` | `char[16]` | Process name (null-terminated, e.g., "pm", "vfs") |
| 82 | `p_endpoint` | `endpoint_t` | Generation-aware endpoint (process identifier for messaging) |
| 84 | `p_sendmsg` | `message` | Message buffer if process is SENDING |
| 85 | `p_delivermsg` | `message` | Message buffer for delivery if MF_DELIVERMSG set |
| 86 | `p_delivermsg_vir` | `vir_bytes` | Virtual address where process wants message delivered |
| 96 | `p_vmrequest.nextrestart` | `struct proc *` | Link in VM restart chain (suspended operation recovery) |
| 97 | `p_vmrequest.nextrequestor` | `struct proc *` | Link in VM request chain (waiting for VM resolution) |
| 103 | `p_vmrequest.type` | `int` | Type of suspended operation (VMSTYPE_SYS_NONE, KERNELCALL, DELIVERMSG, MAP) |
| 106 | `p_vmrequest.saved.reqmsg` | `message` | Copy of original request message that triggered VM intervention |
| 110 | `p_vmrequest.req_type` | `int` | VM request type |
| 111 | `p_vmrequest.target` | `endpoint_t` | Target process endpoint for VM request |
| 114 | `p_vmrequest.params.check.start` | `vir_bytes` | Memory range start address |
| 114 | `p_vmrequest.params.check.length` | `vir_bytes` | Memory range length |
| 115 | `p_vmrequest.params.check.writeflag` | `u8_t` | Nonzero if write access needed |
| 119 | `p_vmrequest.vmresult` | `int` | Result code from VM |
| 126 | `p_found` | `int` | Consistency checking variable |
| 127 | `p_magic` | `int` | Validity check (must equal PMAGIC = 0xC0FFEE1) |
| 132 | `p_defer.r1, r2, r3` | `reg_t` | Deferred system call arguments (if MF_SC_DEFER set) |

#### Architecture-Specific: struct segframe (p_seg)

**x86_64** (minix/include/arch/x86_64/include/archtypes.h:36-41):
- `p_cr3`: `reg_t` — Page table root (PML4 address)
- `p_cr3_v`: `u64_t *` — Virtual pointer to page table
- `fpu_state`: `char *` — FPU register save area
- `p_kern_trap_style`: `int` — Trap handling style indicator

**i386** (minix/include/arch/i386/include/archtypes.h:32-37):
- `p_cr3`: `reg_t` — Page table root address
- `p_cr3_v`: `u32_t *` — Virtual pointer to page table
- `fpu_state`: `char *` — FPU register save area
- `p_kern_trap_style`: `int` — Trap handling style

#### Architecture-Specific: struct stackframe_s (p_reg)

**x86_64** (minix/include/arch/x86_64/include/stackframe.h:14-35):
- `r15, r14, r13, r12, r11, r10, r9, r8`: `reg_t` — 64-bit GPRs (8 each)
- `di, si, fp, bx, dx, cx, retreg`: `reg_t` — Additional GPRs (rdi, rsi, rbp, rbx, rdx, rcx, rax)
- `pc`: `reg_t` — Instruction pointer (rip)
- `cs`: `reg_t` — Code segment
- `psw`: `reg_t` — Processor flags (rflags)
- `sp`: `reg_t` — Stack pointer (rsp)
- `ss`: `reg_t` — Stack segment

**i386** (minix/include/arch/i386/include/stackframe.h:17-36):
- `gs, fs, es, ds`: `u16_t` — Segment registers (saved last by software)
- `di, si, fp, bx, dx, cx, retreg`: `reg_t` — General purpose registers
- `pc, cs, psw, sp, ss`: `reg_t` — CPU trap frame (hardware-saved during interrupt)

---

### 2. RTS_* FLAGS (proc.h:141-166)

Runtime state flags. **Process is runnable iff `p_rts_flags == 0`.**

| Define | Value | Meaning |
|--------|-------|---------|
| `RTS_SLOT_FREE` | 0x01 | Process slot is free/unused |
| `RTS_PROC_STOP` | 0x02 | Process has been stopped (explicit halt) |
| `RTS_SENDING` | 0x04 | Blocked trying to SEND |
| `RTS_RECEIVING` | 0x08 | Blocked trying to RECEIVE |
| `RTS_SIGNALED` | 0x10 | New kernel signal arrived |
| `RTS_SIG_PENDING` | 0x20 | Signal processing in progress |
| `RTS_P_STOP` | 0x40 | Process is being traced (debugger) |
| `RTS_NO_PRIV` | 0x80 | Forked system process waiting for privilege assignment |
| `RTS_NO_ENDPOINT` | 0x100 | Process cannot send/receive (no valid endpoint) |
| `RTS_VMINHIBIT` | 0x200 | Not scheduled until VM sets up page tables |
| `RTS_PAGEFAULT` | 0x400 | Unhandled page fault pending |
| `RTS_VMREQUEST` | 0x800 | Originator of VM memory request (waiting) |
| `RTS_VMREQTARGET` | 0x1000 | Target of VM memory request (helping) |
| `RTS_PREEMPTED` | 0x4000 | Preempted by higher-priority process; requeue at front if runnable |
| `RTS_NO_QUANTUM` | 0x8000 | Ran out of quantum; dequeued, enqueue at end of run queue |
| `RTS_BOOTINHIBIT` | 0x10000 | Not ready until VM completes boot setup |

---

### 3. MF_* FLAGS (proc.h:233-262)

Miscellaneous flags (do not suspend process).

| Define | Value | Meaning |
|--------|-------|---------|
| `MF_REPLY_PEND` | 0x001 | Reply to IPC_REQUEST pending |
| `MF_VIRT_TIMER` | 0x002 | Process-virtual timer is running |
| `MF_PROF_TIMER` | 0x004 | Profile timer is running |
| `MF_KCALL_RESUME` | 0x008 | Kernel call interrupted (VM/page fault); resume needed |
| `MF_DELIVERMSG` | 0x040 | Copy message before process runs |
| `MF_SIG_DELAY` | 0x080 | Send signal when process stops sending |
| `MF_SC_ACTIVE` | 0x100 | Syscall tracing: currently in system call |
| `MF_SC_DEFER` | 0x200 | Syscall tracing: system call deferred (details in p_defer) |
| `MF_SC_TRACE` | 0x400 | Syscall tracing: trigger events |
| `MF_FPU_INITIALIZED` | 0x1000 | Process has used FPU; registers significant |
| `MF_SENDING_FROM_KERNEL` | 0x2000 | Message is from kernel, not user |
| `MF_CONTEXT_SET` | 0x4000 | Don't touch context (being set externally) |
| `MF_SPROF_SEEN` | 0x8000 | Profiling has seen this process |
| `MF_FLUSH_TLB` | 0x10000 | SMP: TLB must be flushed before running |
| `MF_SENDA_VM_MISS` | 0x20000 | Asynchronous receive blocked due to sender VM fault |
| `MF_STEP` | 0x40000 | Single-step process (debugger) |
| `MF_MSGFAILED` | 0x80000 | Message delivery failed |
| `MF_NICED` | 0x100000 | User lowered max process priority |

---

### 4. STRUCT PRIV (minix/kernel/priv.h:21-66)

System privilege structure. Each system process has one; all user processes share one.

| Line | Field | Type | Purpose |
|------|-------|------|---------|
| 22 | `s_proc_nr` | `proc_nr_t` (int) | Associated process table entry number |
| 23 | `s_id` | `sys_id_t` (short) | System privilege ID (index in priv[] array) |
| 24 | `s_flags` | `short` | Privilege flags (PREEMPTIBLE, BILLABLE, DYN_PRIV_ID, SYS_PROC, etc.) |
| 25 | `s_init_flags` | `int` | Initialization flags (e.g., SRV_PROC, SRV_KERN_INFO) |
| 28 | `s_asyntab` | `vir_bytes` | Address of async send table in process address space |
| 29 | `s_asynsize` | `size_t` | Number of elements in async table (0 if not in use) |
| 32 | `s_asynendpoint` | `endpoint_t` | Endpoint that owns async table |
| 34 | `s_trap_mask` | `short` | Bitmask: allowed IPC traps (SEND, RECEIVE, SENDREC, etc.) |
| 35 | `s_ipc_to` | `sys_map_t` | Bitmap: allowed destination processes for IPC |
| 38 | `s_k_call_mask[SYS_CALL_MASK_SIZE]` | `bitchunk_t[]` | Bitmap: allowed kernel calls |
| 40 | `s_sig_mgr` | `endpoint_t` | Signal manager endpoint |
| 41 | `s_bak_sig_mgr` | `endpoint_t` | Backup signal manager endpoint |
| 42 | `s_notify_pending` | `sys_map_t` | Bitmap: pending notification messages |
| 43 | `s_asyn_pending` | `sys_map_t` | Bitmap: pending asynchronous messages |
| 44 | `s_int_pending` | `irq_id_t` | Pending hardware interrupts |
| 45 | `s_sig_pending` | `sigset_t` | Pending signals (standard POSIX signal bitmap) |
| 46 | `s_ipcf` | `ipc_filter_t *` | IPC filter (NULL if none set) |
| 48 | `s_alarm_timer` | `minix_timer_t` | Synchronous alarm timer structure |
| 49 | `s_stack_guard` | `reg_t *` | Stack guard word pointer (kernel tasks only) |
| 51 | `s_diag_sig` | `char` | Send SIGKMESS when diagnostics arrive? |
| 53 | `s_nr_io_range` | `int` | Number of I/O port ranges allowed |
| 54 | `s_io_tab[NR_IO_RANGE]` | `struct io_range[]` | I/O port range table (base/limit pairs) |
| 56 | `s_nr_mem_range` | `int` | Number of memory ranges allowed |
| 57 | `s_mem_tab[NR_MEM_RANGE]` | `struct minix_mem_range[]` | Memory range table (base/limit pairs) |
| 59 | `s_nr_irq` | `int` | Number of IRQ lines allowed |
| 60 | `s_irq_tab[NR_IRQ]` | `int[]` | IRQ line table |
| 61 | `s_grant_table` | `vir_bytes` | Grant table address in process |
| 62 | `s_grant_entries` | `int` | Number of grant table entries |
| 63 | `s_grant_endpoint` | `endpoint_t` | Endpoint that owns grant table |
| 64 | `s_state_table` | `vir_bytes` | State table address in process |
| 65 | `s_state_entries` | `int` | Number of state table entries |

#### s_flags Values (minix/include/minix/const.h:142-154)

| Define | Value | Meaning |
|--------|-------|---------|
| `PREEMPTIBLE` | 0x002 | Process can be preempted (kernel tasks not preemptible) |
| `BILLABLE` | 0x004 | CPU time charged to this process |
| `DYN_PRIV_ID` | 0x008 | Privilege ID assigned dynamically |
| `SYS_PROC` | 0x010 | System process (has own priv structure) |
| `CHECK_IO_PORT` | 0x020 | Check I/O port access requests |
| `CHECK_IRQ` | 0x040 | Check IRQ usage |
| `CHECK_MEM` | 0x080 | Check VM memory mapping requests |
| `ROOT_SYS_PROC` | 0x100 | Root system process instance (RS) |
| `VM_SYS_PROC` | 0x200 | VM system process instance |
| `LU_SYS_PROC` | 0x400 | Live-updated system process (subject to removal in minix-4 port) |
| `RST_SYS_PROC` | 0x800 | Restarted system process |

#### s_trap_mask Values (minix/include/minix/priv.h:58-63)

IPC trap constants (bit positions correspond to IPC call types).

| Define | Value | Trap Allowed |
|--------|-------|--------------|
| `CSK_T` | `(1 << RECEIVE)` | RECEIVE only (CLOCK, SYSTEM tasks) |
| `TSK_T` | `0` | No traps (other kernel tasks) |
| `SRV_T` | `(~0)` | All traps (system services) |
| `DSRV_T` | `(~0)` | All traps (dynamic system services) |
| `USR_T` | `(1 << SENDREC)` | SENDREC only (user processes) |

**IPC Call Type Constants** (minix/include/minix/ipcconst.h:7-13):
- `SEND` = 1
- `RECEIVE` = 2
- `SENDREC` = 3
- `NOTIFY` = 4
- `SENDNB` = 5
- `SENDA` = 16

#### s_ipc_to Bitmap

Type: `sys_map_t` — array of `bitchunk_t` (uint32_t).
- Size: `BITMAP_CHUNKS(NR_SYS_PROCS)` chunks
- `NR_SYS_PROCS` = 64 (from minix/include/minix/sys_config.h:9)
- `BITMAP_CHUNKS(64)` = `(64 + 31) / 32` = 2 chunks (x86_64: 2 × 32 = 64 bits)
- Bit `i` set → can send to process with priv ID `i`
- Helpers: `get_sys_bit(map, bit)`, `set_sys_bit(map, bit)` (kernel/const.h:20-27)

#### s_k_call_mask Bitmap

Type: `bitchunk_t[]` of size `SYS_CALL_MASK_SIZE`.
- `NR_SYS_CALLS` = 58 (minix/include/minix/com.h:270)
- `SYS_CALL_MASK_SIZE` = `BITMAP_CHUNKS(58)` = 2 chunks
- Bit `i` set → kernel call `i` is allowed

---

### 5. BOOT-TIME INITIALIZATION (minix/kernel/table.c & main.c)

#### Boot Image Table (table.c:44-65)

Static array of `struct boot_image`:
```c
struct boot_image image[NR_BOOT_PROCS] = {
  {ASYNCM,        "asyncm"},  /* Async message handler (task) */
  {IDLE,          "idle"  },  /* Idle task */
  {CLOCK,         "clock" },  /* Clock task */
  {SYSTEM,        "system"},  /* System task */
  {HARDWARE,      "kernel"},  /* Hardware task */
  {DS_PROC_NR,    "ds"    },  /* Data Store service */
  {RS_PROC_NR,    "rs"    },  /* Reincarnation Server (root system) */
  {PM_PROC_NR,    "pm"    },  /* Process Manager */
  {SCHED_PROC_NR, "sched" },  /* Scheduler */
  {VFS_PROC_NR,   "vfs"   },  /* Virtual File System */
  {MEM_PROC_NR,   "memory"},  /* Memory driver */
  {TTY_PROC_NR,   "tty"   },  /* TTY driver */
  {MIB_PROC_NR,   "mib"   },  /* MIB service */
  {VM_PROC_NR,    "vm"    },  /* Virtual Memory manager */
  {PFS_PROC_NR,   "pfs"   },  /* Pipe file system */
  {MFS_PROC_NR,   "mfs"   },  /* Minix File System */
  {INIT_PROC_NR,  "init"  },  /* Init (first user process) */
};
```

**Process Number Constants** (minix/include/minix/com.h:47-78):
```c
#define ASYNCM    ((endpoint_t) -5)
#define IDLE      ((endpoint_t) -4)
#define CLOCK     ((endpoint_t) -3)
#define SYSTEM    ((endpoint_t) -2)
#define KERNEL    ((endpoint_t) -1)
#define HARDWARE  KERNEL

#define NR_TASKS  5

#define PM_PROC_NR    ((endpoint_t) 0)
#define VFS_PROC_NR   ((endpoint_t) 1)
#define RS_PROC_NR    ((endpoint_t) 2)
#define MEM_PROC_NR   ((endpoint_t) 3)
#define SCHED_PROC_NR ((endpoint_t) 4)
#define TTY_PROC_NR   ((endpoint_t) 5)
#define DS_PROC_NR    ((endpoint_t) 6)
#define MIB_PROC_NR   ((endpoint_t) 7)
#define VM_PROC_NR    ((endpoint_t) 8)
#define PFS_PROC_NR   ((endpoint_t) 9)
#define MFS_PROC_NR   ((endpoint_t) 10)
#define INIT_PROC_NR  ((endpoint_t) 11)

#define NR_BOOT_PROCS = NR_TASKS + INIT_PROC_NR + 1 = 17
```

#### Privilege Initialization Patterns (main.c:165-254)

Only **kernel tasks** (i < NR_TASKS), **RS (root system process)**, and **VM** run immediately. Others blocked by `RTS_NO_PRIV`.

**Kernel Tasks (ASYNCM, IDLE, CLOCK, SYSTEM)**:
- `s_flags = TSK_F = SYS_PROC` (0x010)
- `s_init_flags = TSK_I = 0`
- `s_trap_mask = TSK_T = 0` (except CLOCK, SYSTEM: `CSK_T = (1 << RECEIVE)`)
- `s_ipc_to = TSK_M = NO_M = -1` (no IPC targets)
- `s_k_call_mask[*] = 0` (no kernel calls)
- IDLE additionally: `s_flags = IDL_F = SYS_PROC | BILLABLE` (0x014)

**VM Process**:
- `s_flags = VM_F = SYS_PROC | VM_SYS_PROC` (0x210)
- `s_trap_mask = SRV_T = (~0)` (all traps allowed)
- `s_ipc_to = SRV_M = ALL_M = -2` (all targets allowed)
- `s_k_call_mask[*] = (~0)` (all kernel calls)
- `s_sig_mgr = SELF`
- Priority: `SRV_Q = USER_Q = 8` (default user priority)
- Quantum: `SRV_QT = USER_QUANTUM = 200` ms

**Root System Process (RS)**:
- `s_flags = RSYS_F = SYS_PROC | PREEMPTIBLE | ROOT_SYS_PROC` (0x116)
- `s_init_flags = SRV_I = 0`
- `s_trap_mask = SRV_T = (~0)`
- `s_ipc_to = SRV_M = ALL_M = -2`
- `s_k_call_mask[*] = (~0)`
- `s_sig_mgr = SRV_SM = RS_PROC_NR = 2`
- Priority: `SRV_Q = 8`
- Quantum: `SRV_QT = 200` ms

**IPC Target Mask Population** (main.c:236-244):
```c
memset(&map, 0, sizeof(map));
if (ipc_to_m == ALL_M) {
    for(j = 0; j < NR_SYS_PROCS; j++)
        set_sys_bit(map, j);  /* Allow to all system processes */
}
fill_sendto_mask(rp, &map);  /* Apply to s_ipc_to */
```

#### Constants & Sizes

**Configuration** (minix/include/minix/sys_config.h:8-9):
- `_NR_PROCS = 256` — user process slots
- `_NR_SYS_PROCS = 64` — system process privilege slots

**Bitmap Constants** (minix/include/minix/bitmap.h:12-13):
- `BITCHUNK_BITS = 32` (sizeof(uint32_t) * CHAR_BIT)
- `BITMAP_CHUNKS(nr_bits) = ((nr_bits) + 31) / 32`

**Task Limits** (minix/include/minix/com.h:54-56):
- `MAX_NR_TASKS = 1023`
- `NR_TASKS = 5`
- Actual boot procs: `NR_BOOT_PROCS = 17`

**I/O & IRQ** (minix/include/minix/config.h:51-58):
- `NR_IO_RANGE = 64` — max I/O port range assignments
- `NR_MEM_RANGE = 20` — max memory range assignments
- `NR_IRQ = 16` — max IRQ lines

---

### 6. TYPE DEFINITIONS

**minix/kernel/type.h:9-13:**
```c
typedef int proc_nr_t;                                    /* process nr */
typedef short sys_id_t;                                   /* system priv id */
typedef struct {
  bitchunk_t chunk[BITMAP_CHUNKS(NR_SYS_PROCS)];
} sys_map_t;
```

**minix/include/minix/type.h:145:**
```c
#define PROC_NAME_LEN 16
```

**sys/sys/types.h:124:**
```c
typedef uint32_t bitchunk_t;
```

**minix/include/minix/timers.h:32-38:**
```c
typedef struct minix_timer {
  struct minix_timer *tmr_next;
  clock_t tmr_exp_time;        /* expiration time (absolute) */
  tmr_func_t tmr_func;         /* callback function */
  int tmr_arg;                 /* integer argument */
} minix_timer_t;
```

**minix/kernel/ipc_filter.h:43-50:**
```c
struct ipc_filter_s {
  int type;                           /* IPCF_NONE/BLACKLIST/WHITELIST */
  int num_elements;
  int flags;
  struct ipc_filter_s *next;
  ipc_filter_el_t elements[IPCF_MAX_ELEMENTS];
};
typedef struct ipc_filter_s ipc_filter_t;
```

---

### 7. STRUCTURE SIZES (Estimated on x86_64)

**sizeof(struct proc):** ~2000+ bytes (highly architecture/config dependent)
- `p_reg` (stackframe_s): 160 bytes (20 × 8 bytes)
- `p_seg` (segframe): ~32 bytes
- Accounting/scheduling: ~200 bytes
- IPC/VM fields: ~400 bytes
- Flags/pointers: ~100 bytes
- SMP-specific (CONFIG_SMP): +256+ bytes for CPU masks

**sizeof(struct priv):** ~800+ bytes
- Bitmaps (s_ipc_to, s_k_call_mask, s_notify_pending, s_asyn_pending): ~16 bytes
- I/O tables (s_io_tab[64]): 512 bytes (8 bytes × 64)
- Memory tables (s_mem_tab[20]): 320 bytes (16 bytes × 20)
- IRQ table (s_irq_tab[16]): 64 bytes
- Timers/pointers/scalars: ~100 bytes

**NR_SYS_PROCS = 64** static priv slots globally allocated.
**NR_TASKS + NR_PROCS = 5 + 256 = 261** process table slots.

No compile-time size assertions found in the codebase; sizes are configuration-dependent and verified at runtime.

---

### 8. NOTES FOR MINIX-4 PORT

**Features to drop (not in minix-4 scope):**
- LIVEUPDATE (LU_SYS_PROC flag and related)
- SMP (CONFIG_SMP, CPU masks, TLB tracking)
- Profiling fields (p_sprof_seen, sprof data)
- Some scheduling complexity (multiple CPUs, migration)

**Features likely retained:**
- Core IPC machinery (s_ipc_to, s_trap_mask, RTS_* flags)
- Privilege isolation (s_flags, s_k_call_mask)
- VM integration (p_vmrequest, RTS_VMINHIBIT)
- Signal/timer handling (s_alarm_timer, p_pending)
- Grant tables (s_grant_table, s_grant_endpoint)
- Process accounting (p_accounting, p_user_time, p_sys_time)

**Simplifications possible:**
- Single-CPU scheduling (drop p_cpu_mask, p_stale_tlb, p_cpu)
- Simpler I/O model (potentially reduce NR_IO_RANGE, NR_MEM_RANGE)
- Streamlined FPU state (p_kern_trap_style may be architecture-specific)
- Reduced debug/tracing overhead (DEBUG_TRACE, p_schedules)

---

This reference captures the complete structure layout, flag semantics, initialization patterns, and sizing information for porting MINIX 3's process and privilege tables to Rust. All line citations are from the MINIX 3 source tree at `/Users/kevinbarnard/src/learn/minix/`.
I have enough context. Before writing the plan, a couple of design choices need your call.

questions.question: How strict should the new ProcNr / SysId / PrivId types be?

options.label: Newtype structs (Recommended)
options.description: Make ProcNr, SysId, PrivId distinct types via `pub struct ProcNr(i32)`. Prevents mixing them up with each other or with raw Endpoint. Costs a bit of boilerplate (From/Into, ordering, fmt) but pays off as IPC code grows.

options.label: Keep as type aliases
options.description: Leave them as `pub type ProcNr = i32` (matches slice 2.1). Less ceremony, but Rust won't catch e.g. passing an Endpoint where a ProcNr is expected.

questions.question: What should slice 2.2 populate in the proc/priv tables?

options.label: Kernel tasks + boot servers, servers blocked (Recommended)
options.description: Fill all 5 kernel tasks (ASYNCM, IDLE, CLOCK, SYSTEM, HARDWARE) AND the 11 boot servers (PM, VFS, RS, MEM, TTY, DS, MFS, VM, PFS, SCHED, INIT) with their names/endpoints/priv slots. Servers get RTS_NO_PRIV set since their address spaces don't exist yet. UART dump shows the full intended layout.

options.label: Kernel tasks only
options.description: Just fill the 5 kernel tasks. Server slots stay as RTS_SLOT_FREE. Smallest scope; defers the per-server trap masks / ipc_to / k_call_mask tables to phase 2.6 when real ELFs load.

options.label: Tasks + servers + a stub test process
options.description: Same as recommended, plus one extra entry that the next slice can use as the source of an SVC. Adds work that 2.3 will redo, so probably unnecessary.

questions.question: Where should the RTS_* / MF_* flag bit constants live?

options.label: kernel/src/proc/flags.rs (Recommended)
options.description: Kernel-only. These are not part of the IPC protocol — userspace never sees them. Keeping them in kernel/ avoids bloating kernel-shared with private state.

options.label: kernel-shared/src/proc_flags.rs
options.description: Cross-crate. Useful only if a future user-space tool (e.g. a `ps` impl) needs to interpret them. Premature for slice 2.2.

questions.question: How should slice 2.2 handle ArchRegisterFrame (real context frame is slice 2.3)?

options.label: Stub struct in arch/aarch64/context.rs (Recommended)
options.description: Define `pub struct ArchRegisterFrame { pub x: [u64; 31], pub sp_el0: u64, pub elr_el1: u64, pub spsr_el1: u64 }` now. Field set matches what slice 2.3's SVC entry will save. No methods on it yet.

options.label: Reuse exception::ExceptionFrame
options.description: Type-alias `ArchRegisterFrame = ExceptionFrame`. They have nearly the same fields. Risk: exception frames and process frames diverge subtly (vector index field is exception-only); reusing now will need a split in 2.3 anyway.

options.label: Omit until slice 2.3
options.description: Have Proc hold a placeholder `[u64; 64]` byte array. Defer the typed frame entirely. Saves design effort but means the proc-table dump can't show register state, and 2.3 will rewrite the field.

content.text: Now I have enough to draft the plan. Let me write it.

input.content:
# Slice 2.2 — Process and Privilege Tables

## Context

Slice 2.1 shipped the `kernel-shared` foundation: `Message`, `Endpoint`, kernel-call numbers, task/server endpoint constants, and `NR_PROCS = 1024`. The kernel itself is still a Phase-1 boot stub that only prints a banner and halts. Slice 2.2 is the next step in Phase 2: introduce the kernel-side `Proc` and `Priv` data structures, populate them at boot with the kernel tasks and boot-server placeholders, and dump the populated tables over UART so we can see end-to-end that the data is correct before slice 2.3 wires up SVC entry and context switch.

This is data-and-types work — no new control-flow paths, no new traps, no async behavior. The kernel still halts after the dump. The milestone for the slice is the UART output of the proc + priv tables.

Two pieces of plan drift to fix while we're here:
- `docs/plan.md:222` says `NR_PROCS = 256`, but `kernel-shared` chose 1024 in slice 2.1. Treat 1024 as authoritative and update the doc.
- The plan never defined where RTS_/MF_/priv-flag constants live. This slice fixes that: kernel-internal flags in `kernel/src/proc/flags.rs`, IPC-contract sizing constants (`NR_SYS_CALLS`, `NR_IO_RANGE`, etc.) in `kernel-shared`.

Reference: MINIX 3 source at `/Users/kevinbarnard/src/learn/minix/minix/kernel/proc.h`, `priv.h`, `table.c`, `main.c` (the port simplifies: no SMP, no LIVEUPDATE, no profiling, no syscall tracing).

## Approach

Three layers of change, in dependency order:

1. **`kernel-shared`** — sizing constants and newtypes that both kernel and (eventually) servers will reference.
2. **`kernel/src/proc/`** — the new `Proc` and `Priv` structs, static tables, init code, and a UART dump helper.
3. **`kernel/src/main.rs`** — call `proc::init()` then `proc::dump_tables(&mut uart)` after the existing banner.

### 1. `kernel-shared` additions

- **`endpoint.rs` — convert `ProcNr` to a newtype.** The `Endpoint` type alias stays (it's wire-format); `ProcNr` and the new `SysId` / `PrivId` become `#[repr(transparent)] pub struct ProcNr(pub i32)` (etc.) with `Copy`, `Eq`, `Ord`, `Debug`, and explicit `const fn new`/`get` accessors. `make_endpoint` takes `(GenNr, ProcNr)`. `endpoint_proc(e) -> ProcNr` instead of `i32`. Update the 21 existing host-side tests to construct `ProcNr::new(n)` where they currently use bare `i32`. Generation stays a type alias for now.
- **`com.rs`** — re-type the task/server proc-number constants as `ProcNr` (`pub const ASYNCM: ProcNr = ProcNr::new(-5);` etc.); keep the `boot_endpoint(p: ProcNr) -> Endpoint` helper.
- **`callnr.rs`** — add `pub const NR_SYS_CALLS: usize = 32;` (sized for the priv k-call bitmask; we have 14 calls today, padding to 32 keeps the mask a single `u32` and leaves growth room).
- **New file `kernel-shared/src/sys_limits.rs`** — exports `NR_IO_RANGE: usize = 16`, `NR_IRQ: usize = 8`, `NR_MEM_RANGE: usize = 8` (the per-priv-slot caps the kernel will enforce; sized down from MINIX 3's 64/16/20 because slice 2.2 will not actually use them yet and we want to bound the Priv struct size). Re-export from `lib.rs`.
- **New tests in `endpoint.rs`** — confirm `ProcNr` round-trips through `make_endpoint`/`endpoint_proc` for both negative (task) and positive slots and for the three sentinels.

### 2. `kernel/src/proc/` module

New directory; files:

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `mod.rs` | Re-exports; defines `init()` and `dump_tables()` entry points |
| `proc_struct.rs` | `Proc` struct definition and `Proc::EMPTY` const |
| `priv_struct.rs` | `Priv` struct definition, `IoRange` / `MemRange` helper structs, `Priv::EMPTY` const |
| `flags.rs` | RTS_*, MF_*, priv-flag (PREEMPTIBLE/BILLABLE/SYS_PROC/...) and trap-mask (USR_T/SRV_T/CSK_T/TSK_T) constants — all kernel-internal |
| `table.rs` | Static `PROC_TABLE` and `PRIV_TABLE`, addr-of accessors, the `image[]` boot table |
| `dump.rs` | `dump_tables(uart)` — formatted UART output of the populated tables |

#### `Proc` (proc_struct.rs)

Match the plan-doc shape but trim MINIX 3's SMP/profiling/livecode fields:

```rust
#[repr(C)]
pub struct Proc {
    pub regs: ArchRegisterFrame,   // stub in slice 2.2 (see arch change below)
    pub nr: ProcNr,
    pub endpoint: Endpoint,
    pub priv_id: Option<PrivId>,
    pub rts_flags: AtomicU32,
    pub misc_flags: u32,
    pub priority: u8,
    pub quantum_ms: u32,
    pub quantum_left: u64,
    pub user_time: u64,
    pub sys_time: u64,

    // IPC linked-list state (Option<ProcNr> per CLAUDE.md; no raw pointers)
    pub caller_q: Option<ProcNr>,
    pub q_link:   Option<ProcNr>,
    pub getfrom_e: Endpoint,
    pub sendto_e:  Endpoint,
    pub send_msg:    Message,
    pub deliver_msg: Message,

    pub name: [u8; 16],
}
```

`Proc::EMPTY` is a `const` initializer with `nr = ProcNr::new(0)`, `endpoint = NONE`, `rts_flags = AtomicU32::new(RTS_SLOT_FREE)`, zeroed message buffers, empty name.

#### `Priv` (priv_struct.rs)

```rust
#[repr(C)]
pub struct Priv {
    pub id: PrivId,
    pub proc_nr: Option<ProcNr>,
    pub flags: u16,
    pub trap_mask: u16,
    pub ipc_to:         [u32; NR_SYS_PROCS / 32],   // bitmap
    pub k_call_mask:    [u32; NR_SYS_CALLS / 32],   // bitmap (= [u32; 1])
    pub notify_pending: [u32; NR_SYS_PROCS / 32],
    pub asyn_pending:   [u32; NR_SYS_PROCS / 32],
    pub sig_mgr: Endpoint,
    pub io_ranges:  ArrayVec<IoRange, NR_IO_RANGE>,
    pub mem_ranges: ArrayVec<MemRange, NR_MEM_RANGE>,
    pub irqs:       ArrayVec<u32, NR_IRQ>,
    pub grant_table: u64,
    pub grant_entries: u32,
}
```

Add `arrayvec = { version = "0.7", default-features = false }` to `kernel/Cargo.toml`. `IoRange`/`MemRange` are `Copy` `{ base: u64, count: u64 }` structs. `Priv::EMPTY` zeroes everything.

#### `flags.rs` — constants only

- `pub const RTS_SLOT_FREE: u32 = 0x01;` through `RTS_BOOTINHIBIT: u32 = 0x10000;` matching MINIX 3 values (slice 2.5 will start consuming `RTS_SENDING`/`RTS_RECEIVING`).
- `pub const MF_DELIVERMSG: u32 = 0x040;` etc. — only the variants that exist in MINIX 3, dropping `MF_FLUSH_TLB` (no SMP) and `MF_SPROF_SEEN` (no profiling).
- `pub const PREEMPTIBLE: u16 = 0x002; pub const BILLABLE: u16 = 0x004; pub const SYS_PROC: u16 = 0x010;` etc. (drop `LU_SYS_PROC`).
- `pub const TSK_T: u16 = 0; pub const CSK_T: u16 = 1 << RECEIVE; pub const USR_T: u16 = 1 << SENDREC; pub const SRV_T: u16 = !0;`

These imports `SEND`/`RECEIVE`/`SENDREC` from `kernel_shared::ipc_const`.

#### `table.rs` — static storage and the boot image

```rust
const N_SLOTS: usize = NR_TASKS + NR_PROCS;   // 5 + 1024 = 1029

#[repr(transparent)]
struct ProcStorage(UnsafeCell<[Proc; N_SLOTS]>);
unsafe impl Sync for ProcStorage {}
static PROC_TABLE: ProcStorage = ProcStorage(UnsafeCell::new([Proc::EMPTY; N_SLOTS]));

#[repr(transparent)]
struct PrivStorage(UnsafeCell<[Priv; NR_SYS_PROCS]>);
unsafe impl Sync for PrivStorage {}
static PRIV_TABLE: PrivStorage = PrivStorage(UnsafeCell::new([Priv::EMPTY; NR_SYS_PROCS]));

pub(crate) unsafe fn proc_slot(nr: ProcNr) -> *mut Proc { ... }
pub(crate) unsafe fn priv_slot(id: PrivId) -> *mut Priv { ... }
```

Boot image (drives `proc::init`):

```rust
struct BootEntry { nr: ProcNr, name: &'static [u8], priv_flags: u16, trap_mask: u16, priority: u8, quantum_ms: u32, runnable: bool }
const IMAGE: [BootEntry; NR_BOOT_PROCS + NR_TASKS] = [
    // Kernel tasks — TSK_F = SYS_PROC, no traps (CLOCK/SYSTEM get CSK_T), runnable
    BootEntry { nr: ASYNCM, name: b"asyncm", priv_flags: SYS_PROC, trap_mask: TSK_T, priority: TASK_Q, quantum_ms: 0, runnable: true },
    BootEntry { nr: IDLE,   name: b"idle",   priv_flags: SYS_PROC | BILLABLE, trap_mask: TSK_T, priority: IDLE_Q, quantum_ms: 0, runnable: true },
    BootEntry { nr: CLOCK,  name: b"clock",  priv_flags: SYS_PROC, trap_mask: CSK_T, priority: TASK_Q, quantum_ms: 0, runnable: true },
    BootEntry { nr: SYSTEM, name: b"system", priv_flags: SYS_PROC, trap_mask: CSK_T, priority: TASK_Q, quantum_ms: 0, runnable: true },
    BootEntry { nr: HARDWARE, name: b"hardware", priv_flags: SYS_PROC, trap_mask: TSK_T, priority: TASK_Q, quantum_ms: 0, runnable: true },
    // Boot servers — SRV_F = SYS_PROC|PREEMPTIBLE, full trap mask, NOT runnable (RTS_NO_PRIV)
    BootEntry { nr: DS_PROC_NR,    name: b"ds",    priv_flags: SYS_PROC | PREEMPTIBLE, trap_mask: SRV_T, priority: SRV_Q, quantum_ms: 200, runnable: false },
    // ... RS, PM, SCHED, VFS, MEM, TTY, VM, PFS, MFS, INIT (RS gets ROOT_SYS_PROC, VM gets VM_SYS_PROC) ...
];
```

`pub fn init()`:
1. Tables are already zeroed (`Proc::EMPTY` everywhere); set the `nr`/`endpoint`/RTS_SLOT_FREE for every slot so endpoints and proc-numbers are valid for indexing.
2. For each `BootEntry`, allocate a priv slot (kernel tasks get fixed low IDs 0..4 matching their proc index; servers get IDs 5..15), fill the Priv struct, write the corresponding Proc fields (name, priv_id, priority, quantum_ms, trap mask via `priv_id`, RTS = 0 if `runnable` else `RTS_NO_PRIV`).
3. For each "all-targets" server (SRV_M), set every bit in `ipc_to`. Kernel tasks get an empty `ipc_to` (they don't initiate IPC from this slice's perspective).
4. Set every bit in `k_call_mask` for SRV_T privs; leave kernel-task privs' k_call_mask zeroed.

#### `dump.rs` — observable milestone

```rust
pub fn dump_tables(uart: &mut impl Write) -> fmt::Result {
    writeln!(uart, "Process table ({} slots, {} active):", N_SLOTS, count_active())?;
    writeln!(uart, "  nr   endpoint    name             priv   rts        flags  prio  q_ms")?;
    for slot in PROC_TABLE.iter() { /* skip RTS_SLOT_FREE */
        writeln!(uart, "  {:>3}  {:#010x}  {:<16}   {:>3}  {}  {}    {:>3}  {:>4}", ...)?;
    }
    writeln!(uart, "Privilege table ({} used / {} total):", count_priv(), NR_SYS_PROCS)?;
    // similar tabular dump: id, proc_nr, flags, trap_mask, ipc_to[0..], k_call_mask
}
```

`rts` and `flags` are decoded into short mnemonic strings (`NOPRIV`, `SEND`, `RECV`, `SLOT`, etc.) for readability — same spirit as the existing `exception.rs` ESR decode.

### 3. Arch stub

Add `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/context.rs`:

```rust
#[repr(C, align(16))]
#[derive(Copy, Clone)]
pub struct ArchRegisterFrame {
    pub x: [u64; 31],   // x0..x30
    pub sp_el0: u64,
    pub elr_el1: u64,
    pub spsr_el1: u64,
}

impl ArchRegisterFrame {
    pub const EMPTY: Self = Self { x: [0; 31], sp_el0: 0, elr_el1: 0, spsr_el1: 0 };
}
```

Re-exported by `arch/aarch64/mod.rs`; `arch/mod.rs` adds `pub use aarch64::ArchRegisterFrame;`. Slice 2.3 will use the same fields when it wires up SVC entry/save.

### 4. `main.rs` wiring

After the existing banner prints, before `arch::halt()`:

```rust
proc::init();
proc::dump_tables(&mut con).ok();
```

That's the entire control-flow change.

### 5. Doc fix

`docs/plan.md:222`: replace `pub const NR_PROCS: usize = 256;` with `pub const NR_PROCS: usize = 1024;` and the prose nearby that says "Static NR_TASKS+NR_PROCS array" stays correct.

Also tick slice 2.2 status when the PR lands (in the same style as the existing slice 2.1 line at `docs/plan.md:406`).

## Critical files

To create:
- `kernel-shared/src/sys_limits.rs`
- `kernel/src/proc/{mod,proc_struct,priv_struct,flags,table,dump}.rs`
- `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/context.rs`

To modify:
- `kernel-shared/src/lib.rs` — add `pub mod sys_limits;` and re-export `PrivId`, `SysId`
- `kernel-shared/src/endpoint.rs` — newtype `ProcNr`, add `PrivId`, `SysId`; update `make_endpoint` / `endpoint_proc` signatures; update tests
- `kernel-shared/src/com.rs` — re-type task/server constants as `ProcNr`
- `kernel-shared/src/callnr.rs` — add `NR_SYS_CALLS`
- `kernel/Cargo.toml` — add `arrayvec` (no_std)
- `kernel/src/main.rs` — call `proc::init()` + `proc::dump_tables()`
- `kernel/src/lib.rs` (or `main.rs` module decls) — `pub mod proc;`
- `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/mod.rs` — `pub mod context; pub use context::ArchRegisterFrame;`
- `kernel/src/arch/mod.rs` — re-export `ArchRegisterFrame`
- `docs/plan.md` — fix `NR_PROCS` value, mark slice 2.2 status when shipping

## Reused / consumed from slice 2.1

- `Message`, `Endpoint`, `GenNr` from `kernel-shared::{message, endpoint}` — unchanged.
- `ANY`, `NONE`, `SELF` sentinels from `kernel-shared::endpoint`.
- Task constants (`ASYNCM`, `IDLE`, `CLOCK`, `SYSTEM`, `HARDWARE`) and server proc-numbers (`PM_PROC_NR`, ..., `INIT_PROC_NR`) from `kernel-shared::com` — re-typed to `ProcNr`.
- `boot_endpoint(p)` helper from `kernel-shared::com:67` — re-typed.
- IPC primitive constants (`SEND`, `RECEIVE`, `SENDREC`, ...) from `kernel-shared::ipc_const` — feed the trap-mask constants.

- Static-state pattern: follow the `UART_BASE` example in `arch/aarch64/uart.rs:28` (single-CPU, no IRQs yet → `UnsafeCell` + `unsafe impl Sync` is enough; no `Mutex` needed).


```
   -3  0xffffbffd  clock              2   ----------  TSK     15     0
   -2  0xffffbffe  system             3   ----------  TSK     15     0

    ...

Privilege table (16 used / 64 total):

   ...
```

toolUseResult.content:
# Slice 2.2 — Process and Privilege Tables

## Context

Slice 2.1 shipped the `kernel-shared` foundation: `Message`, `Endpoint`, kernel-call numbers, task/server endpoint constants, and `NR_PROCS = 1024`. The kernel itself is still a Phase-1 boot stub that only prints a banner and halts. Slice 2.2 is the next step in Phase 2: introduce the kernel-side `Proc` and `Priv` data structures, populate them at boot with the kernel tasks and boot-server placeholders, and dump the populated tables over UART so we can see end-to-end that the data is correct before slice 2.3 wires up SVC entry and context switch.

This is data-and-types work — no new control-flow paths, no new traps, no async behavior. The kernel still halts after the dump. The milestone for the slice is the UART output of the proc + priv tables.

Two pieces of plan drift to fix while we're here:
- `docs/plan.md:222` says `NR_PROCS = 256`, but `kernel-shared` chose 1024 in slice 2.1. Treat 1024 as authoritative and update the doc.
- The plan never defined where RTS_/MF_/priv-flag constants live. This slice fixes that: kernel-internal flags in `kernel/src/proc/flags.rs`, IPC-contract sizing constants (`NR_SYS_CALLS`, `NR_IO_RANGE`, etc.) in `kernel-shared`.

Reference: MINIX 3 source at `/Users/kevinbarnard/src/learn/minix/minix/kernel/proc.h`, `priv.h`, `table.c`, `main.c` (the port simplifies: no SMP, no LIVEUPDATE, no profiling, no syscall tracing).

## Approach

Three layers of change, in dependency order:

1. **`kernel-shared`** — sizing constants and newtypes that both kernel and (eventually) servers will reference.
2. **`kernel/src/proc/`** — the new `Proc` and `Priv` structs, static tables, init code, and a UART dump helper.
3. **`kernel/src/main.rs`** — call `proc::init()` then `proc::dump_tables(&mut uart)` after the existing banner.

### 1. `kernel-shared` additions

- **`endpoint.rs` — convert `ProcNr` to a newtype.** The `Endpoint` type alias stays (it's wire-format); `ProcNr` and the new `SysId` / `PrivId` become `#[repr(transparent)] pub struct ProcNr(pub i32)` (etc.) with `Copy`, `Eq`, `Ord`, `Debug`, and explicit `const fn new`/`get` accessors. `make_endpoint` takes `(GenNr, ProcNr)`. `endpoint_proc(e) -> ProcNr` instead of `i32`. Update the 21 existing host-side tests to construct `ProcNr::new(n)` where they currently use bare `i32`. Generation stays a type alias for now.
- **`com.rs`** — re-type the task/server proc-number constants as `ProcNr` (`pub const ASYNCM: ProcNr = ProcNr::new(-5);` etc.); keep the `boot_endpoint(p: ProcNr) -> Endpoint` helper.
- **`callnr.rs`** — add `pub const NR_SYS_CALLS: usize = 32;` (sized for the priv k-call bitmask; we have 14 calls today, padding to 32 keeps the mask a single `u32` and leaves growth room).
- **New file `kernel-shared/src/sys_limits.rs`** — exports `NR_IO_RANGE: usize = 16`, `NR_IRQ: usize = 8`, `NR_MEM_RANGE: usize = 8` (the per-priv-slot caps the kernel will enforce; sized down from MINIX 3's 64/16/20 because slice 2.2 will not actually use them yet and we want to bound the Priv struct size). Re-export from `lib.rs`.
- **New tests in `endpoint.rs`** — confirm `ProcNr` round-trips through `make_endpoint`/`endpoint_proc` for both negative (task) and positive slots and for the three sentinels.

### 2. `kernel/src/proc/` module

New directory; files:

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `mod.rs` | Re-exports; defines `init()` and `dump_tables()` entry points |
| `proc_struct.rs` | `Proc` struct definition and `Proc::EMPTY` const |
| `priv_struct.rs` | `Priv` struct definition, `IoRange` / `MemRange` helper structs, `Priv::EMPTY` const |
| `flags.rs` | RTS_*, MF_*, priv-flag (PREEMPTIBLE/BILLABLE/SYS_PROC/...) and trap-mask (USR_T/SRV_T/CSK_T/TSK_T) constants — all kernel-internal |
| `table.rs` | Static `PROC_TABLE` and `PRIV_TABLE`, addr-of accessors, the `image[]` boot table |
| `dump.rs` | `dump_tables(uart)` — formatted UART output of the populated tables |

#### `Proc` (proc_struct.rs)

Match the plan-doc shape but trim MINIX 3's SMP/profiling/livecode fields:

```rust

    pub regs: ArchRegisterFrame,   // stub in slice 2.2 (see arch change below)

    pub misc_flags: u32,
    pub priority: u8,
    pub quantum_ms: u32,
    pub quantum_left: u64,
    pub user_time: u64,
    pub sys_time: u64,

    // IPC linked-list state (Option<ProcNr> per CLAUDE.md; no raw pointers)
    pub caller_q: Option<ProcNr>,
    pub q_link:   Option<ProcNr>,
    pub getfrom_e: Endpoint,
    pub sendto_e:  Endpoint,
    pub send_msg:    Message,
    pub deliver_msg: Message,

    pub name: [u8; 16],
}
```

`Proc::EMPTY` is a `const` initializer with `nr = ProcNr::new(0)`, `endpoint = NONE`, `rts_flags = AtomicU32::new(RTS_SLOT_FREE)`, zeroed message buffers, empty name.

input.plan:
# Slice 2.2 — Process and Privilege Tables

## Context

Slice 2.1 shipped the `kernel-shared` foundation: `Message`, `Endpoint`, kernel-call numbers, task/server endpoint constants, and `NR_PROCS = 1024`. The kernel itself is still a Phase-1 boot stub that only prints a banner and halts. Slice 2.2 is the next step in Phase 2: introduce the kernel-side `Proc` and `Priv` data structures, populate them at boot with the kernel tasks and boot-server placeholders, and dump the populated tables over UART so we can see end-to-end that the data is correct before slice 2.3 wires up SVC entry and context switch.

This is data-and-types work — no new control-flow paths, no new traps, no async behavior. The kernel still halts after the dump. The milestone for the slice is the UART output of the proc + priv tables.

Two pieces of plan drift to fix while we're here:
- `docs/plan.md:222` says `NR_PROCS = 256`, but `kernel-shared` chose 1024 in slice 2.1. Treat 1024 as authoritative and update the doc.
- The plan never defined where RTS_/MF_/priv-flag constants live. This slice fixes that: kernel-internal flags in `kernel/src/proc/flags.rs`, IPC-contract sizing constants (`NR_SYS_CALLS`, `NR_IO_RANGE`, etc.) in `kernel-shared`.

Reference: MINIX 3 source at `/Users/kevinbarnard/src/learn/minix/minix/kernel/proc.h`, `priv.h`, `table.c`, `main.c` (the port simplifies: no SMP, no LIVEUPDATE, no profiling, no syscall tracing).

## Approach

Three layers of change, in dependency order:

1. **`kernel-shared`** — sizing constants and newtypes that both kernel and (eventually) servers will reference.
2. **`kernel/src/proc/`** — the new `Proc` and `Priv` structs, static tables, init code, and a UART dump helper.
3. **`kernel/src/main.rs`** — call `proc::init()` then `proc::dump_tables(&mut uart)` after the existing banner.

### 1. `kernel-shared` additions

- **`endpoint.rs` — convert `ProcNr` to a newtype.** The `Endpoint` type alias stays (it's wire-format); `ProcNr` and the new `SysId` / `PrivId` become `#[repr(transparent)] pub struct ProcNr(pub i32)` (etc.) with `Copy`, `Eq`, `Ord`, `Debug`, and explicit `const fn new`/`get` accessors. `make_endpoint` takes `(GenNr, ProcNr)`. `endpoint_proc(e) -> ProcNr` instead of `i32`. Update the 21 existing host-side tests to construct `ProcNr::new(n)` where they currently use bare `i32`. Generation stays a type alias for now.
- **`com.rs`** — re-type the task/server proc-number constants as `ProcNr` (`pub const ASYNCM: ProcNr = ProcNr::new(-5);` etc.); keep the `boot_endpoint(p: ProcNr) -> Endpoint` helper.
- **`callnr.rs`** — add `pub const NR_SYS_CALLS: usize = 32;` (sized for the priv k-call bitmask; we have 14 calls today, padding to 32 keeps the mask a single `u32` and leaves growth room).
- **New file `kernel-shared/src/sys_limits.rs`** — exports `NR_IO_RANGE: usize = 16`, `NR_IRQ: usize = 8`, `NR_MEM_RANGE: usize = 8` (the per-priv-slot caps the kernel will enforce; sized down from MINIX 3's 64/16/20 because slice 2.2 will not actually use them yet and we want to bound the Priv struct size). Re-export from `lib.rs`.
- **New tests in `endpoint.rs`** — confirm `ProcNr` round-trips through `make_endpoint`/`endpoint_proc` for both negative (task) and positive slots and for the three sentinels.

### 2. `kernel/src/proc/` module

New directory; files:

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `mod.rs` | Re-exports; defines `init()` and `dump_tables()` entry points |
| `proc_struct.rs` | `Proc` struct definition and `Proc::EMPTY` const |
| `priv_struct.rs` | `Priv` struct definition, `IoRange` / `MemRange` helper structs, `Priv::EMPTY` const |
| `flags.rs` | RTS_*, MF_*, priv-flag (PREEMPTIBLE/BILLABLE/SYS_PROC/...) and trap-mask (USR_T/SRV_T/CSK_T/TSK_T) constants — all kernel-internal |
| `table.rs` | Static `PROC_TABLE` and `PRIV_TABLE`, addr-of accessors, the `image[]` boot table |
| `dump.rs` | `dump_tables(uart)` — formatted UART output of the populated tables |

#### `Proc` (proc_struct.rs)

Match the plan-doc shape but trim MINIX 3's SMP/profiling/livecode fields:

```rust
#[repr(C)]
pub struct Proc {
    pub regs: ArchRegisterFrame,   // stub in slice 2.2 (see arch change below)
    pub nr: ProcNr,
    pub endpoint: Endpoint,
    pub priv_id: Option<PrivId>,
    pub rts_flags: AtomicU32,
    pub misc_flags: u32,
    pub priority: u8,
    pub quantum_ms: u32,
    pub quantum_left: u64,
    pub user_time: u64,
    pub sys_time: u64,

    // IPC linked-list state (Option<ProcNr> per CLAUDE.md; no raw pointers)
    pub caller_q: Option<ProcNr>,
    pub q_link:   Option<ProcNr>,
    pub getfrom_e: Endpoint,
    pub sendto_e:  Endpoint,
    pub send_msg:    Message,
    pub deliver_msg: Message,

    pub name: [u8; 16],
}
```

`Proc::EMPTY` is a `const` initializer with `nr = ProcNr::new(0)`, `endpoint = NONE`, `rts_flags = AtomicU32::new(RTS_SLOT_FREE)`, zeroed message buffers, empty name.

#### `Priv` (priv_struct.rs)

```rust
#[repr(C)]
pub struct Priv {
    pub id: PrivId,
    pub proc_nr: Option<ProcNr>,
    pub flags: u16,
    pub trap_mask: u16,
    pub ipc_to:         [u32; NR_SYS_PROCS / 32],   // bitmap
    pub k_call_mask:    [u32; NR_SYS_CALLS / 32],   // bitmap (= [u32; 1])
    pub notify_pending: [u32; NR_SYS_PROCS / 32],
    pub asyn_pending:   [u32; NR_SYS_PROCS / 32],
    pub sig_mgr: Endpoint,
    pub io_ranges:  ArrayVec<IoRange, NR_IO_RANGE>,
    pub mem_ranges: ArrayVec<MemRange, NR_MEM_RANGE>,
    pub irqs:       ArrayVec<u32, NR_IRQ>,
    pub grant_table: u64,
    pub grant_entries: u32,
}
```

Add `arrayvec = { version = "0.7", default-features = false }` to `kernel/Cargo.toml`. `IoRange`/`MemRange` are `Copy` `{ base: u64, count: u64 }` structs. `Priv::EMPTY` zeroes everything.

#### `flags.rs` — constants only

- `pub const RTS_SLOT_FREE: u32 = 0x01;` through `RTS_BOOTINHIBIT: u32 = 0x10000;` matching MINIX 3 values (slice 2.5 will start consuming `RTS_SENDING`/`RTS_RECEIVING`).
- `pub const MF_DELIVERMSG: u32 = 0x040;` etc. — only the variants that exist in MINIX 3, dropping `MF_FLUSH_TLB` (no SMP) and `MF_SPROF_SEEN` (no profiling).
- `pub const PREEMPTIBLE: u16 = 0x002; pub const BILLABLE: u16 = 0x004; pub const SYS_PROC: u16 = 0x010;` etc. (drop `LU_SYS_PROC`).
- `pub const TSK_T: u16 = 0; pub const CSK_T: u16 = 1 << RECEIVE; pub const USR_T: u16 = 1 << SENDREC; pub const SRV_T: u16 = !0;`

These imports `SEND`/`RECEIVE`/`SENDREC` from `kernel_shared::ipc_const`.

#### `table.rs` — static storage and the boot image

```rust
const N_SLOTS: usize = NR_TASKS + NR_PROCS;   // 5 + 1024 = 1029

#[repr(transparent)]
struct ProcStorage(UnsafeCell<[Proc; N_SLOTS]>);
unsafe impl Sync for ProcStorage {}
static PROC_TABLE: ProcStorage = ProcStorage(UnsafeCell::new([Proc::EMPTY; N_SLOTS]));

#[repr(transparent)]
struct PrivStorage(UnsafeCell<[Priv; NR_SYS_PROCS]>);
unsafe impl Sync for PrivStorage {}
static PRIV_TABLE: PrivStorage = PrivStorage(UnsafeCell::new([Priv::EMPTY; NR_SYS_PROCS]));

pub(crate) unsafe fn proc_slot(nr: ProcNr) -> *mut Proc { ... }
pub(crate) unsafe fn priv_slot(id: PrivId) -> *mut Priv { ... }
```

Boot image (drives `proc::init`):

```rust
struct BootEntry { nr: ProcNr, name: &'static [u8], priv_flags: u16, trap_mask: u16, priority: u8, quantum_ms: u32, runnable: bool }
const IMAGE: [BootEntry; NR_BOOT_PROCS + NR_TASKS] = [
    // Kernel tasks — TSK_F = SYS_PROC, no traps (CLOCK/SYSTEM get CSK_T), runnable
    BootEntry { nr: ASYNCM, name: b"asyncm", priv_flags: SYS_PROC, trap_mask: TSK_T, priority: TASK_Q, quantum_ms: 0, runnable: true },
    BootEntry { nr: IDLE,   name: b"idle",   priv_flags: SYS_PROC | BILLABLE, trap_mask: TSK_T, priority: IDLE_Q, quantum_ms: 0, runnable: true },
    BootEntry { nr: CLOCK,  name: b"clock",  priv_flags: SYS_PROC, trap_mask: CSK_T, priority: TASK_Q, quantum_ms: 0, runnable: true },
    BootEntry { nr: SYSTEM, name: b"system", priv_flags: SYS_PROC, trap_mask: CSK_T, priority: TASK_Q, quantum_ms: 0, runnable: true },
    BootEntry { nr: HARDWARE, name: b"hardware", priv_flags: SYS_PROC, trap_mask: TSK_T, priority: TASK_Q, quantum_ms: 0, runnable: true },
    // Boot servers — SRV_F = SYS_PROC|PREEMPTIBLE, full trap mask, NOT runnable (RTS_NO_PRIV)
    BootEntry { nr: DS_PROC_NR,    name: b"ds",    priv_flags: SYS_PROC | PREEMPTIBLE, trap_mask: SRV_T, priority: SRV_Q, quantum_ms: 200, runnable: false },
    // ... RS, PM, SCHED, VFS, MEM, TTY, VM, PFS, MFS, INIT (RS gets ROOT_SYS_PROC, VM gets VM_SYS_PROC) ...
];
```

`pub fn init()`:
1. Tables are already zeroed (`Proc::EMPTY` everywhere); set the `nr`/`endpoint`/RTS_SLOT_FREE for every slot so endpoints and proc-numbers are valid for indexing.
2. For each `BootEntry`, allocate a priv slot (kernel tasks get fixed low IDs 0..4 matching their proc index; servers get IDs 5..15), fill the Priv struct, write the corresponding Proc fields (name, priv_id, priority, quantum_ms, trap mask via `priv_id`, RTS = 0 if `runnable` else `RTS_NO_PRIV`).
3. For each "all-targets" server (SRV_M), set every bit in `ipc_to`. Kernel tasks get an empty `ipc_to` (they don't initiate IPC from this slice's perspective).
4. Set every bit in `k_call_mask` for SRV_T privs; leave kernel-task privs' k_call_mask zeroed.

#### `dump.rs` — observable milestone

```rust
pub fn dump_tables(uart: &mut impl Write) -> fmt::Result {
    writeln!(uart, "Process table ({} slots, {} active):", N_SLOTS, count_active())?;
    writeln!(uart, "  nr   endpoint    name             priv   rts        flags  prio  q_ms")?;
    for slot in PROC_TABLE.iter() { /* skip RTS_SLOT_FREE */
        writeln!(uart, "  {:>3}  {:#010x}  {:<16}   {:>3}  {}  {}    {:>3}  {:>4}", ...)?;
    }
    writeln!(uart, "Privilege table ({} used / {} total):", count_priv(), NR_SYS_PROCS)?;
    // similar tabular dump: id, proc_nr, flags, trap_mask, ipc_to[0..], k_call_mask
}
```

`rts` and `flags` are decoded into short mnemonic strings (`NOPRIV`, `SEND`, `RECV`, `SLOT`, etc.) for readability — same spirit as the existing `exception.rs` ESR decode.

### 3. Arch stub

Add `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/context.rs`:

```rust
#[repr(C, align(16))]
#[derive(Copy, Clone)]
pub struct ArchRegisterFrame {
    pub x: [u64; 31],   // x0..x30
    pub sp_el0: u64,
    pub elr_el1: u64,
    pub spsr_el1: u64,
}

impl ArchRegisterFrame {
    pub const EMPTY: Self = Self { x: [0; 31], sp_el0: 0, elr_el1: 0, spsr_el1: 0 };
}
```

Re-exported by `arch/aarch64/mod.rs`; `arch/mod.rs` adds `pub use aarch64::ArchRegisterFrame;`. Slice 2.3 will use the same fields when it wires up SVC entry/save.

### 4. `main.rs` wiring

After the existing banner prints, before `arch::halt()`:

```rust
proc::init();
proc::dump_tables(&mut con).ok();
```

That's the entire control-flow change.

### 5. Doc fix

`docs/plan.md:222`: replace `pub const NR_PROCS: usize = 256;` with `pub const NR_PROCS: usize = 1024;` and the prose nearby that says "Static NR_TASKS+NR_PROCS array" stays correct.

- `kernel-shared/src/sys_limits.rs`

- `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/context.rs`

- **`callnr.rs`** — add `pub const NR_SYS_CALLS: usize = 32;` (sized for the priv k-call bitmask; we have 14 calls today, padding to 32 keeps the mask a single `u32` and leaves growth room).
- **New file `kernel-shared/src/sys_limits.rs`** — exports `NR_IO_RANGE: usize = 16`, `NR_IRQ: usize = 8`, `NR_MEM_RANGE: usize = 8` (the per-priv-slot caps the kernel will enforce; sized down from MINIX 3's 64/16/20 because slice 2.2 will not actually use them yet and we want to bound the Priv struct size). Re-export from `lib.rs`.
- **New tests in `endpoint.rs`** — confirm `ProcNr` round-trips through `make_endpoint`/`endpoint_proc` for both negative (task) and positive slots and for the three sentinels.

### 2. `kernel/src/proc/` module

New directory; files:

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `mod.rs` | Re-exports; defines `init()` and `dump_tables()` entry points |
| `proc_struct.rs` | `Proc` struct definition and `Proc::EMPTY` const |
| `priv_struct.rs` | `Priv` struct definition, `IoRange` / `MemRange` helper structs, `Priv::EMPTY` const |
| `flags.rs` | RTS_*, MF_*, priv-flag (PREEMPTIBLE/BILLABLE/SYS_PROC/...) and trap-mask (USR_T/SRV_T/CSK_T/TSK_T) constants — all kernel-internal |
| `table.rs` | Static `PROC_TABLE` and `PRIV_TABLE`, addr-of accessors, the `image[]` boot table |
| `dump.rs` | `dump_tables(uart)` — formatted UART output of the populated tables |

#### `Proc` (proc_struct.rs)

Match the plan-doc shape but trim MINIX 3's SMP/profiling/livecode fields:

```rust
#[repr(C)]
pub struct Proc {
    pub regs: ArchRegisterFrame,   // stub in slice 2.2 (see arch change below)
    pub nr: ProcNr,
    pub endpoint: Endpoint,
    pub priv_id: Option<PrivId>,
    pub rts_flags: AtomicU32,
    pub misc_flags: u32,
    pub priority: u8,
    pub quantum_ms: u32,
    pub quantum_left: u64,
    pub user_time: u64,
    pub sys_time: u64,

    // IPC linked-list state (Option<ProcNr> per CLAUDE.md; no raw pointers)
    pub caller_q: Option<ProcNr>,
    pub q_link:   Option<ProcNr>,
    pub getfrom_e: Endpoint,
    pub sendto_e:  Endpoint,
    pub send_msg:    Message,
    pub deliver_msg: Message,

    pub name: [u8; 16],
}
```

`Proc::EMPTY` is a `const` initializer with `nr = ProcNr::new(0)`, `endpoint = NONE`, `rts_flags = AtomicU32::new(RTS_SLOT_FREE)`, zeroed message buffers, empty name.

#### `Priv` (priv_struct.rs)

```rust
#[repr(C)]
pub struct Priv {
    pub id: PrivId,
    pub proc_nr: Option<ProcNr>,
    pub flags: u16,
    pub trap_mask: u16,
    pub ipc_to:         [u32; NR_SYS_PROCS / 32],   // bitmap
    pub k_call_mask:    [u32; NR_SYS_CALLS / 32],   // bitmap (= [u32; 1])
    pub notify_pending: [u32; NR_SYS_PROCS / 32],
    pub asyn_pending:   [u32; NR_SYS_PROCS / 32],
    pub sig_mgr: Endpoint,
    pub io_ranges:  ArrayVec<IoRange, NR_IO_RANGE>,
    pub mem_ranges: ArrayVec<MemRange, NR_MEM_RANGE>,
    pub irqs:       ArrayVec<u32, NR_IRQ>,
    pub grant_table: u64,
    pub grant_entries: u32,
}
```

Add `arrayvec = { version = "0.7", default-features = false }` to `kernel/Cargo.toml`. `IoRange`/`MemRange` are `Copy` `{ base: u64, count: u64 }` structs. `Priv::EMPTY` zeroes everything.

#### `flags.rs` — constants only

- `pub const RTS_SLOT_FREE: u32 = 0x01;` through `RTS_BOOTINHIBIT: u32 = 0x10000;` matching MINIX 3 values (slice 2.5 will start consuming `RTS_SENDING`/`RTS_RECEIVING`).
- `pub const MF_DELIVERMSG: u32 = 0x040;` etc. — only the variants that exist in MINIX 3, dropping `MF_FLUSH_TLB` (no SMP) and `MF_SPROF_SEEN` (no profiling).
- `pub const PREEMPTIBLE: u16 = 0x002; pub const BILLABLE: u16 = 0x004; pub const SYS_PROC: u16 = 0x010;` etc. (drop `LU_SYS_PROC`).
- `pub const TSK_T: u16 = 0; pub const CSK_T: u16 = 1 << RECEIVE; pub const USR_T: u16 = 1 << SENDREC; pub const SRV_T: u16 = !0;`

These imports `SEND`/`RECEIVE`/`SENDREC` from `kernel_shared::ipc_const`.

#### `table.rs` — static storage and the boot image

```rust
const N_SLOTS: usize = NR_TASKS + NR_PROCS;   // 5 + 1024 = 1029

#[repr(transparent)]
struct ProcStorage(UnsafeCell<[Proc; N_SLOTS]>);
unsafe impl Sync for ProcStorage {}
static PROC_TABLE: ProcStorage = ProcStorage(UnsafeCell::new([Proc::EMPTY; N_SLOTS]));

#[repr(transparent)]
struct PrivStorage(UnsafeCell<[Priv; NR_SYS_PROCS]>);
unsafe impl Sync for PrivStorage {}
static PRIV_TABLE: PrivStorage = PrivStorage(UnsafeCell::new([Priv::EMPTY; NR_SYS_PROCS]));

pub(crate) unsafe fn proc_slot(nr: ProcNr) -> *mut Proc { ... }
pub(crate) unsafe fn priv_slot(id: PrivId) -> *mut Priv { ... }
```

Boot image (drives `proc::init`):

```rust
struct BootEntry { nr: ProcNr, name: &'static [u8], priv_flags: u16, trap_mask: u16, priority: u8, quantum_ms: u32, runnable: bool }
const IMAGE: [BootEntry; NR_BOOT_PROCS + NR_TASKS] = [
    // Kernel tasks — TSK_F = SYS_PROC, no traps (CLOCK/SYSTEM get CSK_T), runnable
    BootEntry { nr: ASYNCM, name: b"asyncm", priv_flags: SYS_PROC, trap_mask: TSK_T, priority: TASK_Q, quantum_ms: 0, runnable: true },
    BootEntry { nr: IDLE,   name: b"idle",   priv_flags: SYS_PROC | BILLABLE, trap_mask: TSK_T, priority: IDLE_Q, quantum_ms: 0, runnable: true },
    BootEntry { nr: CLOCK,  name: b"clock",  priv_flags: SYS_PROC, trap_mask: CSK_T, priority: TASK_Q, quantum_ms: 0, runnable: true },
    BootEntry { nr: SYSTEM, name: b"system", priv_flags: SYS_PROC, trap_mask: CSK_T, priority: TASK_Q, quantum_ms: 0, runnable: true },
    BootEntry { nr: HARDWARE, name: b"hardware", priv_flags: SYS_PROC, trap_mask: TSK_T, priority: TASK_Q, quantum_ms: 0, runnable: true },
    // Boot servers — SRV_F = SYS_PROC|PREEMPTIBLE, full trap mask, NOT runnable (RTS_NO_PRIV)
    BootEntry { nr: DS_PROC_NR,    name: b"ds",    priv_flags: SYS_PROC | PREEMPTIBLE, trap_mask: SRV_T, priority: SRV_Q, quantum_ms: 200, runnable: false },
    // ... RS, PM, SCHED, VFS, MEM, TTY, VM, PFS, MFS, INIT (RS gets ROOT_SYS_PROC, VM gets VM_SYS_PROC) ...
];
```

`pub fn init()`:
1. Tables are already zeroed (`Proc::EMPTY` everywhere); set the `nr`/`endpoint`/RTS_SLOT_FREE for every slot so endpoints and proc-numbers are valid for indexing.
2. For each `BootEntry`, allocate a priv slot (kernel tasks get fixed low IDs 0..4 matching their proc index; servers get IDs 5..15), fill the Priv struct, write the corresponding Proc fields (name, priv_id, priority, quantum_ms, trap mask via `priv_id`, RTS = 0 if `runnable` else `RTS_NO_PRIV`).
3. For each "all-targets" server (SRV_M), set every bit in `ipc_to`. Kernel tasks get an empty `ipc_to` (they don't initiate IPC from this slice's perspective).
4. Set every bit in `k_call_mask` for SRV_T privs; leave kernel-task privs' k_call_mask zeroed.

#### `dump.rs` — observable milestone

```rust
pub fn dump_tables(uart: &mut impl Write) -> fmt::Result {
    writeln!(uart, "Process table ({} slots, {} active):", N_SLOTS, count_active())?;
    writeln!(uart, "  nr   endpoint    name             priv   rts        flags  prio  q_ms")?;
    for slot in PROC_TABLE.iter() { /* skip RTS_SLOT_FREE */
        writeln!(uart, "  {:>3}  {:#010x}  {:<16}   {:>3}  {}  {}    {:>3}  {:>4}", ...)?;
    }
    writeln!(uart, "Privilege table ({} used / {} total):", count_priv(), NR_SYS_PROCS)?;
    // similar tabular dump: id, proc_nr, flags, trap_mask, ipc_to[0..], k_call_mask
}
```

`rts` and `flags` are decoded into short mnemonic strings (`NOPRIV`, `SEND`, `RECV`, `SLOT`, etc.) for readability — same spirit as the existing `exception.rs` ESR decode.

### 3. Arch stub

Add `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/context.rs`:

```rust
#[repr(C, align(16))]
#[derive(Copy, Clone)]
pub struct ArchRegisterFrame {
    pub x: [u64; 31],   // x0..x30
    pub sp_el0: u64,
    pub elr_el1: u64,
    pub spsr_el1: u64,
}

impl ArchRegisterFrame {
    pub const EMPTY: Self = Self { x: [0; 31], sp_el0: 0, elr_el1: 0, spsr_el1: 0 };
}
```

Re-exported by `arch/aarch64/mod.rs`; `arch/mod.rs` adds `pub use aarch64::ArchRegisterFrame;`. Slice 2.3 will use the same fields when it wires up SVC entry/save.

### 4. `main.rs` wiring

After the existing banner prints, before `arch::halt()`:

```rust
proc::init();
proc::dump_tables(&mut con).ok();
```

That's the entire control-flow change.

### 5. Doc fix

`docs/plan.md:222`: replace `pub const NR_PROCS: usize = 256;` with `pub const NR_PROCS: usize = 1024;` and the prose nearby that says "Static NR_TASKS+NR_PROCS array" stays correct.

Also tick slice 2.2 status when the PR lands (in the same style as the existing slice 2.1 line at `docs/plan.md:406`).

## Critical files

To create:
- `kernel-shared/src/sys_limits.rs`
- `kernel/src/proc/{mod,proc_struct,priv_struct,flags,table,dump}.rs`
- `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/context.rs`

To modify:
- `kernel-shared/src/lib.rs` — add `pub mod sys_limits;` and re-export `PrivId`, `SysId`
- `kernel-shared/src/endpoint.rs` — newtype `ProcNr`, add `PrivId`, `SysId`; update `make_endpoint` / `endpoint_proc` signatures; update tests
- `kernel-shared/src/com.rs` — re-type task/server constants as `ProcNr`
- `kernel-shared/src/callnr.rs` — add `NR_SYS_CALLS`
- `kernel/Cargo.toml` — add `arrayvec` (no_std)
- `kernel/src/main.rs` — call `proc::init()` + `proc::dump_tables()`
- `kernel/src/lib.rs` (or `main.rs` module decls) — `pub mod proc;`
- `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/mod.rs` — `pub mod context; pub use context::ArchRegisterFrame;`
- `kernel/src/arch/mod.rs` — re-export `ArchRegisterFrame`
- `docs/plan.md` — fix `NR_PROCS` value, mark slice 2.2 status when shipping

## Reused / consumed from slice 2.1

- `Message`, `Endpoint`, `GenNr` from `kernel-shared::{message, endpoint}` — unchanged.
- `ANY`, `NONE`, `SELF` sentinels from `kernel-shared::endpoint`.
- Task constants (`ASYNCM`, `IDLE`, `CLOCK`, `SYSTEM`, `HARDWARE`) and server proc-numbers (`PM_PROC_NR`, ..., `INIT_PROC_NR`) from `kernel-shared::com` — re-typed to `ProcNr`.
- `boot_endpoint(p)` helper from `kernel-shared::com:67` — re-typed.

- UART print path: `arch::Uart::new()` + `core::fmt::Write` (matches the pattern already used in `main.rs:33-43` and `panic.rs:11-13`).
- Static-state pattern: follow the `UART_BASE` example in `arch/aarch64/uart.rs:28` (single-CPU, no IRQs yet → `UnsafeCell` + `unsafe impl Sync` is enough; no `Mutex` needed).

## Verification

Host-side (cheap, runs in CI / `cargo test -p minix4-kernel-shared`):
- All 21 existing endpoint tests pass after the `ProcNr` newtype migration.
- Add ~10 new tests: `ProcNr::new` round-trips through `make_endpoint`/`endpoint_proc`, the three sentinels still decode to their expected slots, `NR_SYS_CALLS` matches the number of `SYS_*` constants in `callnr.rs`.

Kernel-side (QEMU, the slice milestone):
```bash

tools/qemu-run.sh              # via the cargo runner
```
Expected UART output (banner first, then proc/priv dump, then halt):
```
MINIX 4 booting on aarch64
HHDM offset: 0x...
Process table (1029 slots, 16 active):
  nr   endpoint    name             priv   rts        flags  prio  q_ms
   -5  0xffffbffb  asyncm             0   ----------  TSK     15     0
   -4  0xffffbffc  idle               1   ----------  TSK     15     0
   -3  0xffffbffd  clock              2   ----------  TSK     15     0
```

(Exact numeric formatting may vary; the key checks are: 5 kernel tasks shown as runnable with TSK flags, 11 boot servers shown with `NOPRIV` and `SRV` flags, each server has its `ipc_to` bitmap set to "all 16 active slots", and the priv-id assignments are stable and contiguous.)

toolUseResult.plan:
# Slice 2.2 — Process and Privilege Tables

## Context

Slice 2.1 shipped the `kernel-shared` foundation: `Message`, `Endpoint`, kernel-call numbers, task/server endpoint constants, and `NR_PROCS = 1024`. The kernel itself is still a Phase-1 boot stub that only prints a banner and halts. Slice 2.2 is the next step in Phase 2: introduce the kernel-side `Proc` and `Priv` data structures, populate them at boot with the kernel tasks and boot-server placeholders, and dump the populated tables over UART so we can see end-to-end that the data is correct before slice 2.3 wires up SVC entry and context switch.

This is data-and-types work — no new control-flow paths, no new traps, no async behavior. The kernel still halts after the dump. The milestone for the slice is the UART output of the proc + priv tables.

Two pieces of plan drift to fix while we're here:
- `docs/plan.md:222` says `NR_PROCS = 256`, but `kernel-shared` chose 1024 in slice 2.1. Treat 1024 as authoritative and update the doc.
- The plan never defined where RTS_/MF_/priv-flag constants live. This slice fixes that: kernel-internal flags in `kernel/src/proc/flags.rs`, IPC-contract sizing constants (`NR_SYS_CALLS`, `NR_IO_RANGE`, etc.) in `kernel-shared`.

Reference: MINIX 3 source at `/Users/kevinbarnard/src/learn/minix/minix/kernel/proc.h`, `priv.h`, `table.c`, `main.c` (the port simplifies: no SMP, no LIVEUPDATE, no profiling, no syscall tracing).

## Approach

Three layers of change, in dependency order:

1. **`kernel-shared`** — sizing constants and newtypes that both kernel and (eventually) servers will reference.
2. **`kernel/src/proc/`** — the new `Proc` and `Priv` structs, static tables, init code, and a UART dump helper.
3. **`kernel/src/main.rs`** — call `proc::init()` then `proc::dump_tables(&mut uart)` after the existing banner.

### 1. `kernel-shared` additions

- **`endpoint.rs` — convert `ProcNr` to a newtype.** The `Endpoint` type alias stays (it's wire-format); `ProcNr` and the new `SysId` / `PrivId` become `#[repr(transparent)] pub struct ProcNr(pub i32)` (etc.) with `Copy`, `Eq`, `Ord`, `Debug`, and explicit `const fn new`/`get` accessors. `make_endpoint` takes `(GenNr, ProcNr)`. `endpoint_proc(e) -> ProcNr` instead of `i32`. Update the 21 existing host-side tests to construct `ProcNr::new(n)` where they currently use bare `i32`. Generation stays a type alias for now.
- **`com.rs`** — re-type the task/server proc-number constants as `ProcNr` (`pub const ASYNCM: ProcNr = ProcNr::new(-5);` etc.); keep the `boot_endpoint(p: ProcNr) -> Endpoint` helper.
- **`callnr.rs`** — add `pub const NR_SYS_CALLS: usize = 32;` (sized for the priv k-call bitmask; we have 14 calls today, padding to 32 keeps the mask a single `u32` and leaves growth room).
- **New file `kernel-shared/src/sys_limits.rs`** — exports `NR_IO_RANGE: usize = 16`, `NR_IRQ: usize = 8`, `NR_MEM_RANGE: usize = 8` (the per-priv-slot caps the kernel will enforce; sized down from MINIX 3's 64/16/20 because slice 2.2 will not actually use them yet and we want to bound the Priv struct size). Re-export from `lib.rs`.
- **New tests in `endpoint.rs`** — confirm `ProcNr` round-trips through `make_endpoint`/`endpoint_proc` for both negative (task) and positive slots and for the three sentinels.

### 2. `kernel/src/proc/` module

New directory; files:

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `mod.rs` | Re-exports; defines `init()` and `dump_tables()` entry points |
| `proc_struct.rs` | `Proc` struct definition and `Proc::EMPTY` const |
| `priv_struct.rs` | `Priv` struct definition, `IoRange` / `MemRange` helper structs, `Priv::EMPTY` const |
| `flags.rs` | RTS_*, MF_*, priv-flag (PREEMPTIBLE/BILLABLE/SYS_PROC/...) and trap-mask (USR_T/SRV_T/CSK_T/TSK_T) constants — all kernel-internal |
| `table.rs` | Static `PROC_TABLE` and `PRIV_TABLE`, addr-of accessors, the `image[]` boot table |
| `dump.rs` | `dump_tables(uart)` — formatted UART output of the populated tables |

#### `Proc` (proc_struct.rs)

Match the plan-doc shape but trim MINIX 3's SMP/profiling/livecode fields:


#### `table.rs` — static storage and the boot image

```rust

    BootEntry { nr: HARDWARE, name: b"hardware", priv_flags: SYS_PROC, trap_mask: TSK_T, priority: TASK_Q, quantum_ms: 0, runnable: true },
    // Boot servers — SRV_F = SYS_PROC|PREEMPTIBLE, full trap mask, NOT runnable (RTS_NO_PRIV)

    // ... RS, PM, SCHED, VFS, MEM, TTY, VM, PFS, MFS, INIT (RS gets ROOT_SYS_PROC, VM gets VM_SYS_PROC) ...
```

`pub fn init()`:

2. For each `BootEntry`, allocate a priv slot (kernel tasks get fixed low IDs 0..4 matching their proc index; servers get IDs 5..15), fill the Priv struct, write the corresponding Proc fields (name, priv_id, priority, quantum_ms, trap mask via `priv_id`, RTS = 0 if `runnable` else `RTS_NO_PRIV`).
3. For each "all-targets" server (SRV_M), set every bit in `ipc_to`. Kernel tasks get an empty `ipc_to` (they don't initiate IPC from this slice's perspective).
4. Set every bit in `k_call_mask` for SRV_T privs; leave kernel-task privs' k_call_mask zeroed.

#### `dump.rs` — observable milestone

```rust
pub fn dump_tables(uart: &mut impl Write) -> fmt::Result {
    writeln!(uart, "Process table ({} slots, {} active):", N_SLOTS, count_active())?;
    writeln!(uart, "  nr   endpoint    name             priv   rts        flags  prio  q_ms")?;
    for slot in PROC_TABLE.iter() { /* skip RTS_SLOT_FREE */
        writeln!(uart, "  {:>3}  {:#010x}  {:<16}   {:>3}  {}  {}    {:>3}  {:>4}", ...)?;
    }
    writeln!(uart, "Privilege table ({} used / {} total):", count_priv(), NR_SYS_PROCS)?;
    // similar tabular dump: id, proc_nr, flags, trap_mask, ipc_to[0..], k_call_mask
}
```

`rts` and `flags` are decoded into short mnemonic strings (`NOPRIV`, `SEND`, `RECV`, `SLOT`, etc.) for readability — same spirit as the existing `exception.rs` ESR decode.

### 3. Arch stub

Add `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/context.rs`:

```rust
#[repr(C, align(16))]
#[derive(Copy, Clone)]
pub struct ArchRegisterFrame {
    pub x: [u64; 31],   // x0..x30
    pub sp_el0: u64,
    pub elr_el1: u64,
    pub spsr_el1: u64,
}

impl ArchRegisterFrame {
    pub const EMPTY: Self = Self { x: [0; 31], sp_el0: 0, elr_el1: 0, spsr_el1: 0 };
}
```

Re-exported by `arch/aarch64/mod.rs`; `arch/mod.rs` adds `pub use aarch64::ArchRegisterFrame;`. Slice 2.3 will use the same fields when it wires up SVC entry/save.

### 4. `main.rs` wiring

After the existing banner prints, before `arch::halt()`:

```rust
proc::init();
proc::dump_tables(&mut con).ok();
```

That's the entire control-flow change.

### 5. Doc fix

`docs/plan.md:222`: replace `pub const NR_PROCS: usize = 256;` with `pub const NR_PROCS: usize = 1024;` and the prose nearby that says "Static NR_TASKS+NR_PROCS array" stays correct.

Also tick slice 2.2 status when the PR lands (in the same style as the existing slice 2.1 line at `docs/plan.md:406`).

## Critical files

To create:
- `kernel-shared/src/sys_limits.rs`
- `kernel/src/proc/{mod,proc_struct,priv_struct,flags,table,dump}.rs`
- `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/context.rs`

To modify:
- `kernel-shared/src/lib.rs` — add `pub mod sys_limits;` and re-export `PrivId`, `SysId`
- `kernel-shared/src/endpoint.rs` — newtype `ProcNr`, add `PrivId`, `SysId`; update `make_endpoint` / `endpoint_proc` signatures; update tests
- `kernel-shared/src/com.rs` — re-type task/server constants as `ProcNr`
- `kernel-shared/src/callnr.rs` — add `NR_SYS_CALLS`
- `kernel/Cargo.toml` — add `arrayvec` (no_std)
- `kernel/src/main.rs` — call `proc::init()` + `proc::dump_tables()`
- `kernel/src/lib.rs` (or `main.rs` module decls) — `pub mod proc;`
- `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/mod.rs` — `pub mod context; pub use context::ArchRegisterFrame;`
- `kernel/src/arch/mod.rs` — re-export `ArchRegisterFrame`
- `docs/plan.md` — fix `NR_PROCS` value, mark slice 2.2 status when shipping

## Reused / consumed from slice 2.1

- `Message`, `Endpoint`, `GenNr` from `kernel-shared::{message, endpoint}` — unchanged.
- `ANY`, `NONE`, `SELF` sentinels from `kernel-shared::endpoint`.
- Task constants (`ASYNCM`, `IDLE`, `CLOCK`, `SYSTEM`, `HARDWARE`) and server proc-numbers (`PM_PROC_NR`, ..., `INIT_PROC_NR`) from `kernel-shared::com` — re-typed to `ProcNr`.
- `boot_endpoint(p)` helper from `kernel-shared::com:67` — re-typed.
- IPC primitive constants (`SEND`, `RECEIVE`, `SENDREC`, ...) from `kernel-shared::ipc_const` — feed the trap-mask constants.
- UART print path: `arch::Uart::new()` + `core::fmt::Write` (matches the pattern already used in `main.rs:33-43` and `panic.rs:11-13`).
- Static-state pattern: follow the `UART_BASE` example in `arch/aarch64/uart.rs:28` (single-CPU, no IRQs yet → `UnsafeCell` + `unsafe impl Sync` is enough; no `Mutex` needed).

## Verification

Host-side (cheap, runs in CI / `cargo test -p minix4-kernel-shared`):
- All 21 existing endpoint tests pass after the `ProcNr` newtype migration.
- Add ~10 new tests: `ProcNr::new` round-trips through `make_endpoint`/`endpoint_proc`, the three sentinels still decode to their expected slots, `NR_SYS_CALLS` matches the number of `SYS_*` constants in `callnr.rs`.

Kernel-side (QEMU, the slice milestone):
```bash
cargo kernel-aarch64           # builds clean (no warnings)
tools/qemu-run.sh              # via the cargo runner
```
Expected UART output (banner first, then proc/priv dump, then halt):
```
MINIX 4 booting on aarch64
HHDM offset: 0x...
Process table (1029 slots, 16 active):
  nr   endpoint    name             priv   rts        flags  prio  q_ms
   -5  0xffffbffb  asyncm             0   ----------  TSK     15     0
   -4  0xffffbffc  idle               1   ----------  TSK     15     0
   -3  0xffffbffd  clock              2   ----------  TSK     15     0
   -2  0xffffbffe  system             3   ----------  TSK     15     0
   -1  0xffffbfff  hardware           4   ----------  TSK     15     0
    0  0x00000000  pm                 7   NOPRIV      SRV      8   200
    1  0x00000001  vfs                9   NOPRIV      SRV      8   200
    2  0x00000002  rs                 6   NOPRIV     RSRV      4   200
    ...
   10  0x0000000a  init              16   NOPRIV      SRV     14   200
Privilege table (16 used / 64 total):
  id   proc  flags   trap_mask  ipc_to[0..1]              k_call
   0   asyncm  0x010  0x0000     0x0000_0000 0x0000_0000   0x000_0000
   ...
```
(Exact numeric formatting may vary; the key checks are: 5 kernel tasks shown as runnable with TSK flags, 11 boot servers shown with `NOPRIV` and `SRV` flags, each server has its `ipc_to` bitmap set to "all 16 active slots", and the priv-id assignments are stable and contiguous.)

Verify by:
1. Eyeballing the dump matches the boot image — every entry from `IMAGE` is present with the right name/priv-flags.
2. Injecting a deliberate fault before `proc::init()` and confirming the existing panic dump still works (regression check on slice 2.1 behavior).
3. Verifying `cargo kernel-x86_64` still fails with the expected "unsupported target arch: x86_64" message — we did not regress the alias resolution.

End-to-end smoke: nothing observable changes beyond the new UART block. Phase 2 milestone (ping-pong) lands at slice 2.5; this slice only proves the data layout.

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	#
3	# tools/qemu-run.sh -- cargo runner for the aarch64 kernel target.
4	#
5	# Cargo invokes this with the kernel ELF path as $1. We build a tiny ESP
6	# directory tree on the fly and hand it to QEMU's `-drive file=fat:rw:DIR`
7	# helper, which exposes the directory as a FAT32 filesystem. That avoids
8	# needing parted/mtools/hdiutil to land a "MINIX 4 booting" banner.
9	#
10	# Override the UEFI firmware location with QEMU_EFI_AARCH64=/path/to/fw.fd.
11	
12	set -euo pipefail
13	
14	if [[ $# -lt 1 ]]; then
15	    echo "usage: $0 <kernel-elf> [extra qemu args...]" >&2
16	    exit 64
17	fi
18	
19	KERNEL="$1"
20	shift
21	
22	ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
23	ESP="$ROOT/target/esp"
24	LIMINE_BIN="$ROOT/external/limine/dist/BOOTAA64.EFI"
25	
26	# Materialize the Limine binary if `make` hasn't been run yet.
27	if [[ ! -f "$LIMINE_BIN" ]]; then
28	    echo "==> Fetching Limine binaries (one-time)..." >&2
29	    make -C "$ROOT/external/limine" >&2
30	fi
31	
32	# Locate aarch64 UEFI firmware. Honor explicit override first.
33	FIRMWARE_CANDIDATES=(
34	    "${QEMU_EFI_AARCH64:-}"
35	    "$(brew --prefix qemu 2>/dev/null || true)/share/qemu/edk2-aarch64-code.fd"
36	    "/opt/homebrew/share/qemu/edk2-aarch64-code.fd"
37	    "/usr/local/share/qemu/edk2-aarch64-code.fd"
38	    "/usr/share/edk2-aarch64/QEMU_EFI.fd"
39	    "/usr/share/AAVMF/AAVMF_CODE.fd"
40	)
41	FIRMWARE=""
42	for cand in "${FIRMWARE_CANDIDATES[@]}"; do
43	    if [[ -n "$cand" && -f "$cand" ]]; then
44	        FIRMWARE="$cand"
45	        break
46	    fi
47	done
48	if [[ -z "$FIRMWARE" ]]; then
49	    cat >&2 <<'EOF'
50	error: no aarch64 UEFI firmware found.
51	       Install QEMU (brew install qemu) or point QEMU_EFI_AARCH64 at an
52	       OVMF/edk2 firmware blob, e.g.:
53	           export QEMU_EFI_AARCH64=/path/to/QEMU_EFI.fd
54	EOF
55	    exit 69
56	fi
57	
58	# Stage the ESP directory tree.
59	rm -rf "$ESP"
60	mkdir -p "$ESP/EFI/BOOT" "$ESP/boot"
61	cp "$LIMINE_BIN" "$ESP/EFI/BOOT/BOOTAA64.EFI"
62	cp "$ROOT/tools/limine.conf" "$ESP/limine.conf"
63	cp "$KERNEL" "$ESP/boot/kernel"
64	
65	exec qemu-system-aarch64 \
66	    -M virt -cpu cortex-a72 -m 256M \
67	    -bios "$FIRMWARE" \
68	    -drive "file=fat:rw:fat-type=32:$ESP,format=raw,if=virtio" \
69	    -display none \
70	    -serial stdio \
71	    -no-reboot \
72	    "$@"
73

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# tools/qemu-run.sh -- cargo runner for the aarch64 kernel target.
#
# Cargo invokes this with the kernel ELF path as $1. We build a tiny ESP
# directory tree on the fly and hand it to QEMU's `-drive file=fat:rw:DIR`
# helper, which exposes the directory as a FAT32 filesystem. That avoids
# needing parted/mtools/hdiutil to land a "MINIX 4 booting" banner.
#
# Override the UEFI firmware location with QEMU_EFI_AARCH64=/path/to/fw.fd.

set -euo pipefail

if [[ $# -lt 1 ]]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <kernel-elf> [extra qemu args...]" >&2
    exit 64
fi

KERNEL="$1"
shift

ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
ESP="$ROOT/target/esp"
LIMINE_BIN="$ROOT/external/limine/dist/BOOTAA64.EFI"

# Materialize the Limine binary if `make` hasn't been run yet.
if [[ ! -f "$LIMINE_BIN" ]]; then
    echo "==> Fetching Limine binaries (one-time)..." >&2
    make -C "$ROOT/external/limine" >&2
fi

# Locate aarch64 UEFI firmware. Honor explicit override first.
FIRMWARE_CANDIDATES=(
    "${QEMU_EFI_AARCH64:-}"
    "$(brew --prefix qemu 2>/dev/null || true)/share/qemu/edk2-aarch64-code.fd"
    "/opt/homebrew/share/qemu/edk2-aarch64-code.fd"
    "/usr/local/share/qemu/edk2-aarch64-code.fd"
    "/usr/share/edk2-aarch64/QEMU_EFI.fd"
    "/usr/share/AAVMF/AAVMF_CODE.fd"
)
FIRMWARE=""
for cand in "${FIRMWARE_CANDIDATES[@]}"; do
    if [[ -n "$cand" && -f "$cand" ]]; then
        FIRMWARE="$cand"
        break
    fi
done
if [[ -z "$FIRMWARE" ]]; then
    cat >&2 <<'EOF'
error: no aarch64 UEFI firmware found.
       Install QEMU (brew install qemu) or point QEMU_EFI_AARCH64 at an
       OVMF/edk2 firmware blob, e.g.:
           export QEMU_EFI_AARCH64=/path/to/QEMU_EFI.fd
EOF
    exit 69
fi

# Stage the ESP directory tree.
rm -rf "$ESP"
mkdir -p "$ESP/EFI/BOOT" "$ESP/boot"
cp "$LIMINE_BIN" "$ESP/EFI/BOOT/BOOTAA64.EFI"
cp "$ROOT/tools/limine.conf" "$ESP/limine.conf"
cp "$KERNEL" "$ESP/boot/kernel"

exec qemu-system-aarch64 \
    -M virt -cpu cortex-a72 -m 256M \
    -bios "$FIRMWARE" \
    -drive "file=fat:rw:fat-type=32:$ESP,format=raw,if=virtio" \
    -display none \
    -serial stdio \
    -no-reboot \
    "$@"
